title: adenocalymma molle
intro: Adenocalymma molle, also known as the "soft dog flower," is a plant species that belongs to the Bignoniaceae family and is native to the tropical regions of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains alkaloids and flavonoids with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Adenocalymma molle include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
